items.headline: Library parcel tax rates increase for FY 2026-27
items.summary: San José City Council approved an increase to library parcel tax rates for the 2026-27 fiscal year. The rate change will affect property owners who fund the library system through this dedicated tax.

items.headline: Eight new homes proposed on Wolfe Road lot
items.summary: A proposal to demolish an existing single-family house at 781 S Wolfe Road and build eight two-story homes on the 0.69-acre site is scheduled for Planning Commission review. The project would subdivide the land into nine lots with a 65.8% floor area ratio.

items.headline: 136-unit apartment complex permitted near Trimble Road
items.summary: Trimble Modular Apts receives permit for 136 units at 110 Yoshino Way, a $22 million residential project. The development adds rental housing capacity in the Trimble corridor area of San José.

items.headline: Los Gatos to hold municipal election November 3, 2026
items.summary: City Council adopted a resolution calling a general municipal election for three positions on November 3, 2026. This sets the formal date for voters to elect representatives to fill those council seats.

items.headline: Final map approved for Tract 10675 Boulevard development
items.summary: City Council adopted a resolution approving the final map for Tract 10675, a subdivision project located on Boulevard. The approval clears the way for the subdivision to proceed.

items.headline: Community Development Block Grant funding planned for FY 2026-27
items.summary: Cupertino City Council discussed the Fiscal Year 2026-27 Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) program. CDBG funds support community development, housing, and social services projects in the city.

items.headline: Parks and Recreation Strategic Plan Adopted
items.summary: Mountain View City Council adopted a new Parks and Recreation Strategic Plan at the May 26 meeting. The plan sets direction for parks and recreation services in the city.

items.headline: Council to Consider Five-Year City Decarbonization Plan
items.summary: On May 26, the City Council held a study session to review the city's decarbonization goal and a proposed five-year plan with specific approach and actions. A regular vote or decision on the plan was not indicated in the agenda.
